title: Portal Access Application System with ID/Password Login

requirements.text: In the Motoko backend, add a new stable data type `PortalAccessApplication` containing fields: applicantName (Text), email (Text), role (#student | #staff | #parent | #admin), programmeOrDepartment (Text, optional context), submittedAt (Int timestamp), status (#pending | #approved | #rejected), and generatedId (Text, the role-specific ID assigned on approval, e.g. matriculation number for students, staff ID for staff, parent ID for parents, admin ID for admins). Add a `generatedPassword` (Text) field to store the hashed or plaintext generated password. Store applications in a stable HashMap keyed by a unique application ID (Text). Implement the following backend functions: `submitPortalAccessApplication(name, email, role, programme) -> Result<Text, Text>` (returns assigned application reference), `approvePortalAccessApplication(appId) -> Result<{generatedId: Text; password: Text}, Text>` (admin-only, generates a role-specific ID and random password, stores them, marks status approved), `rejectPortalAccessApplication(appId) -> Result<(), Text>` (admin-only), `getPortalAccessApplications() -> [PortalAccessApplication]` (admin-only), `getMyPortalAccessApplication(email: Text) -> ?PortalAccessApplication` (public query by email), and `loginWithIdAndPassword(roleId: Text, password: Text) -> Result<UserProfile, Text>` (validates credentials and returns the associated profile).

quotes: create a portal access application system where students, staff, parents, and admins can apply for access to their respective portals
After registration, generate login details using their Staff ID, Student Matriculation Number, Parent ID, or Admin ID along with a password

acceptanceCriteria: A `PortalAccessApplication` type exists with all specified fields in stable storage.
`submitPortalAccessApplication` stores a new application with status #pending and returns a reference ID.

`approvePortalAccessApplication` (admin-only) generates a unique role-specific ID (e.g., MATRIC/2024/0001 for students, STAFF-0001 for staff, PARENT-0001 for parents, ADMIN-0001 for admins) and a random alphanumeric password, stores them on the application record, and marks the application approved.
`rejectPortalAccessApplication` marks the application rejected and returns an error if called by non-admin.
`getPortalAccessApplications` returns all applications and is restricted to admin callers.
`getMyPortalAccessApplication` allows any caller to look up their application by email.
`loginWithIdAndPassword` returns the matching UserProfile when roleId and password match an approved application, and returns an error otherwise.

requirements.text: Create a public-facing Portal Access Application page at `/apply` (`frontend/src/pages/PortalAccessApplicationPage.tsx`). The page must display the university logo and heading 'Apply for Portal Access'. It must render a single-step form with fields: Full Name (text), Email Address (text), Role (dropdown: Student, Staff, Parent, Admin), and Programme/Department (text, labelled contextually based on role — 'Programme' for students, 'Department' for staff/admin, 'Ward's Programme' for parents). On submission, call the `submitPortalAccessApplication` backend function via a React Query mutation. On success, display a confirmation message: 'Your application has been submitted. You will receive your login credentials once your access is approved by the administrator.' On error, display a human-readable inline error message. The page must use the existing deep navy and gold academic theme.

quotes: students, staff, parents, and admins can apply for access to their respective portals

acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/apply` without authentication.
The form collects Full Name, Email, Role, and Programme/Department.
Submitting the form calls the backend `submitPortalAccessApplication` function.
A success confirmation message is shown on successful submission.
Inline error messages are shown on failure.
The page uses the existing deep navy and gold theme with the university logo visible.

requirements.text: Add an 'Access Applications' section to the Admin Panel (`frontend/src/pages/admin/AccessApplicationsPage.tsx`) wrapped with RoleGuard (admin role). This page must fetch all portal access applications via `getPortalAccessApplications` and display them in a table with columns: Applicant Name, Email, Role, Programme/Department, Submitted Date, Status (badge), and an Actions column. For pending applications, show 'Approve' and 'Reject' buttons. Clicking 'Approve' calls `approvePortalAccessApplication` and on success shows a modal/toast displaying the generated Role ID and Password so the admin can communicate these credentials to the applicant (e.g., 'Student ID: MATRIC/2024/0001 | Password: Xk7#mP2q'). Clicking 'Reject' calls `rejectPortalAccessApplication`. After any action, immediately invalidate and refetch the applications list query. Add a link to this page in the Admin Dashboard sidebar under the label 'Access Applications'.

quotes: After registration, generate login details using their Staff ID, Student Matriculation Number, Parent ID, or Admin ID along with a password

acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/admin/access-applications` and is wrapped with RoleGuard requiring admin role.
All submitted applications are listed in a table with Name, Email, Role, Programme, Date, Status columns.
Pending applications show 'Approve' and 'Reject' action buttons.
Approving an application displays the generated Role ID and Password in a prominent modal or toast.
Rejecting an application updates the status to 'Rejected' immediately in the list.
The Admin sidebar includes a link labelled 'Access Applications' pointing to this page.
React Query list is invalidated and refetched after approve/reject actions.

requirements.text: Create a dedicated ID/Password login page at `/id-login` (`frontend/src/pages/IdPasswordLoginPage.tsx`). This page must prominently display the university logo (from `/assets/generated/university-logo.dim_200x200.png` or the existing logo asset), the university name, and a login form with two fields: 'Your ID' (placeholder text changes based on a role selector: 'Enter Matriculation Number' for students, 'Enter Staff ID' for staff, 'Enter Parent ID' for parents, 'Enter Admin ID' for admins) and 'Password'. Include a Role selector (Student, Staff, Admin, Parent) above the ID field so the placeholder updates dynamically. On form submission, call the `loginWithIdAndPassword` backend function. On success, store the returned UserProfile in React Query state/context and redirect the user to their role-specific dashboard (`/student`, `/staff`, `/admin`, or `/parent`). On failure, display a clear inline error: 'Invalid ID or password. Please check your credentials or contact the administrator.' The page must use the deep navy and gold academic theme and be fully responsive.

quotes: redirect users to a login page with the school logo where they can enter their ID and password to access their specific dashboard
Once registered, redirect users to a login page with the school logo

acceptanceCriteria: The page is accessible at `/id-login` without authentication.
The university logo and name are displayed prominently at the top of the login form.
A Role selector (Student, Staff, Admin, Parent) is present and updates the ID field placeholder text accordingly.
Submitting the form calls `loginWithIdAndPassword` with the entered ID and password.
On success, the user is redirected to their role dashboard (`/student`, `/staff`, `/admin`, or `/parent`).
On failure, an inline error message 'Invalid ID or password. Please check your credentials or contact the administrator.' is shown.
The page is styled with the deep navy and gold academic theme and is mobile responsive.

requirements.text: Add an 'Apply for Portal Access' link and an 'ID/Password Login' link to the public navigation and the Portal Selection page (`/portal`). On the public homepage (`HomePage.tsx`), add a visible call-to-action banner or card section titled 'New User? Apply for Portal Access' with a button linking to `/apply` and a secondary link 'Already have credentials? Login with your ID' linking to `/id-login`. On the Portal Selection page (`PortalSelectionPage.tsx`), add a footer row beneath the four portal cards with these two links. In the main Navigation component, add an 'Apply for Access' link and a 'ID Login' link in the unauthenticated public navigation links.

quotes: students, staff, parents, and admins can apply for access to their respective portals
redirect users to a login page with the school logo

acceptanceCriteria: The public homepage displays a banner or card section with a button linking to `/apply`.
The homepage also shows a secondary link to `/id-login` for users who already have credentials.
The Portal Selection page at `/portal` shows links to both `/apply` and `/id-login` beneath the four role cards.
The top navigation bar shows 'Apply for Access' and 'ID Login' links to unauthenticated visitors.

requirements.text: Add React Query hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for the new portal access application backend functions: `useSubmitPortalAccessApplication` (mutation calling `submitPortalAccessApplication`), `useGetPortalAccessApplications` (query calling `getPortalAccessApplications`, for admin use), `useApprovePortalAccessApplication` (mutation calling `approvePortalAccessApplication`), `useRejectPortalAccessApplication` (mutation calling `rejectPortalAccessApplication`), `useGetMyPortalAccessApplication(email)` (query calling `getMyPortalAccessApplication`), and `useLoginWithIdAndPassword` (mutation calling `loginWithIdAndPassword`). Each hook must follow the existing patterns in `useQueries.ts` — using the authenticated actor for admin-only mutations and the anonymous actor for public queries/mutations.

quotes: generate login details using their Staff ID, Student Matriculation Number, Parent ID, or Admin ID along with a password

acceptanceCriteria: `useSubmitPortalAccessApplication` mutation hook exists and calls the backend function correctly.
`useGetPortalAccessApplications` query hook exists, uses the authenticated actor, and is usable on the admin Access Applications page.
`useApprovePortalAccessApplication` and `useRejectPortalAccessApplication` mutation hooks exist, use the authenticated actor, and invalidate the applications list query on success.
`useGetMyPortalAccessApplication` query hook exists and uses the anonymous actor.
`useLoginWithIdAndPassword` mutation hook exists and uses the anonymous actor.
All hooks follow existing naming conventions and patterns in `useQueries.ts`.

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
The ID/password login system must coexist with the existing Internet Identity login — do not remove or break existing II-based authentication flows.
Generated role-specific IDs must follow a consistent, human-readable format (e.g., MATRIC/2024/XXXX for students, STAFF-XXXX for staff, PARENT-XXXX for parents, ADMIN-XXXX for admins).
The loginWithIdAndPassword function must not require Internet Identity — it is a separate credential-based authentication path.
nonGoals: Do not remove or replace the existing Internet Identity login flow.
Do not send email notifications — credential display is admin-to-applicant manual communication.
Do not implement SMS or push notification delivery of credentials.
Do not change the existing RoleGuard, useInternetIdentity, or useActor hook implementations.
